author: doll-agent
content:
Research outcome: keep concrete-types architecture; defer dispatch protocol indefinitely.

The numpy story: __array_function__ is a runtime dispatch hook that lets Dask/CuPy/JAX intercept numpy function calls. Works because Python is duck-typed.

Why this doesn't translate to Rust:
1. Rust's monomorphization model is the opposite of runtime dispatch. Backend selection is conventionally done at the type system (generic over a trait), which is more powerful (zero-overhead, type-safe, optimizable per backend) and doesn't need a protocol.
2. The 'same code runs on numpy/dask/cupy' use case is a Python interpreter-shape concern. Rust users already write generic code over traits.

An ArrayLike trait would need ~30 methods (shape, dtype, indexing, slicing, etc.); most ferray ops would need rewriting generic over ArrayLike, doubling the API surface.

The right path for a multi-backend ferray future: build specific backend trait families when concrete demand appears (ferray-cuda, ferray-lazy as their own crates with compatible-but-distinct types). Architecture is more like Polars+DataFusion than numpy+dask.

Closing as decided. Revisit if a credible Rust-native generic-array protocol emerges in the wider ecosystem.
